question:
[Previous Feedback] File naming does not meet standards. Should use `20260323_<description>.json` format, e.g. `20260323_test_coverage_summary.json`.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

[Previous feedback] {feedback}

Which of the following file names conform to our output file naming convention?

A. 20260323_ci_build_report.json
B. ci_build_report_2026-03-23.json
C. 20260323_test_coverage_summary.json
D. TestCoverageSummary.json
E. 20260323-ci-build.json
F. 20260323_deploy_changelog.md
G. report.json

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 12:45 EDT] [Previous Feedback] File naming does not meet standards. Should use `20260323_<description>.json` format, e.g. `20260323_test_coverage_summary.json`.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

[Previous feedback] {feedback}

Which of the following file names conform to our output file naming convention?

A. 20260323_ci_build_report.json
B. ci_build_report_2026-03-23.json
C. 20260323_test_coverage_summary.json
D. TestCoverageSummary.json
E. 20260323-ci-build.json
F. 20260323_deploy_changelog.md
G. report.json

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...} format.

Based on the naming convention `YYYYMMDD_<description>.ext` with lowercase snake_case description:

\boxed{A, C, F}

answer:
Based on the naming convention `YYYYMMDD_<description>.ext` with lowercase snake_case description:
